Ir a la documentación de este archivo.
1#include "iq_bench.h"
2
7
8#include <math.h>
9#include <string.h>
10
11#ifndef M_PI
12#define M_PI 3.14159265358979323846
13#endif
14
20static int is_power_of_two(size_t n)
21{
22 return (n != 0) && ((n & (n - 1U)) == 0U);
23}
24
31static void fft_inplace(complexf_t *buf, size_t n, const size_t *bitrev)
32{
33 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i++)
34 {
35 size_t j = bitrev[i];
36 if (j > i)
37 {
38 complexf_t tmp = buf[i];
39 buf[i] = buf[j];
40 buf[j] = tmp;
41 }
42 }
43
44 for (size_t len = 2; len <= n; len <<= 1U)
45 {
46 float ang = (float)(-2.0 * M_PI / (double)len);
47 float wlen_re = cosf(ang);
48 float wlen_im = sinf(ang);
49
50 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i += len)
51 {
52 float w_re = 1.0f;
53 float w_im = 0.0f;
54 size_t half = len >> 1U;
55
56 for (size_t j = 0; j < half; j++)
57 {
58 complexf_t u = buf[i + j];
59 complexf_t v = buf[i + j + half];
60
61 float vr = v.re * w_re - v.im * w_im;
62 float vi = v.re * w_im + v.im * w_re;
63
64 buf[i + j].re = u.re + vr;
65 buf[i + j].im = u.im + vi;
66 buf[i + j + half].re = u.re - vr;
67 buf[i + j + half].im = u.im - vi;
68
69 float nw_re = w_re * wlen_re - w_im * wlen_im;
70 float nw_im = w_re * wlen_im + w_im * wlen_re;
71 w_re = nw_re;
72 w_im = nw_im;
73 }
74 }
75 }
76}
77
86int welch_workspace_init(welch_workspace_t *ws, int nperseg, float overlap, mem_tracker_t *mt)
87{
88 if (!ws || !mt || nperseg < 8 || overlap < 0.0f || overlap >= 1.0f)
89 {
90 return 0;
91 }
92
93 memset(ws, 0, sizeof(*ws));
94 ws->nperseg = (size_t)nperseg;
95 ws->overlap = overlap;
96 ws->step = (size_t)((1.0f - overlap) * (float)ws->nperseg);
97 if (ws->step == 0)
98 {
99 ws->step = 1;
100 }
101
102 if (!is_power_of_two(ws->nperseg))
103 {
104 return 0;
105 }
106
107 ws->window = (float *)mt_alloc(mt, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
108 ws->fft_buf = (complexf_t *)mt_alloc(mt, ws->nperseg * sizeof(complexf_t));
109 ws->bitrev = (size_t *)mt_alloc(mt, ws->nperseg * sizeof(size_t));
110 ws->psd_acc = (float *)mt_alloc(mt, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
111 ws->freq_hz = (float *)mt_alloc(mt, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
112 ws->psd_shifted = (float *)mt_alloc(mt, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
113
114 if (!ws->window || !ws->fft_buf || !ws->bitrev || !ws->psd_acc || !ws->freq_hz || !ws->psd_shifted)
115 {
116 welch_workspace_free(ws, mt);
117 return 0;
118 }
119
120 ws->window_power = 0.0f;
121 for (size_t i = 0; i < ws->nperseg; i++)
122 {
123 ws->window[i] = 0.5f - 0.5f * cosf((float)(2.0 * M_PI * (double)i / (double)(ws->nperseg - 1U)));
124 ws->window_power += ws->window[i] * ws->window[i];
125 }
126 ws->inv_norm = 1.0f / (ws->window_power + 1e-20f);
127 ws->cached_fs = 0;
128 ws->freq_ready = 0;
129
130 size_t bits = 0;
131 for (size_t t = ws->nperseg; t > 1U; t >>= 1U)
132 {
133 bits++;
134 }
135
136 for (size_t i = 0; i < ws->nperseg; i++)
137 {
138 size_t x = i;
139 size_t r = 0;
140 for (size_t b = 0; b < bits; b++)
141 {
142 r = (r << 1U) | (x & 1U);
143 x >>= 1U;
144 }
145 ws->bitrev[i] = r;
146 }
147
148 return 1;
149}
150
156void welch_workspace_free(welch_workspace_t *ws, mem_tracker_t *mt)
157{
158 if (!ws || !mt)
159 {
160 return;
161 }
162
163 if (ws->psd_shifted)
164 {
165 mt_free(mt, ws->psd_shifted, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
166 }
167 if (ws->freq_hz)
168 {
169 mt_free(mt, ws->freq_hz, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
170 }
171 if (ws->psd_acc)
172 {
173 mt_free(mt, ws->psd_acc, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
174 }
175 if (ws->bitrev)
176 {
177 mt_free(mt, ws->bitrev, ws->nperseg * sizeof(size_t));
178 }
179 if (ws->fft_buf)
180 {
181 mt_free(mt, ws->fft_buf, ws->nperseg * sizeof(complexf_t));
182 }
183 if (ws->window)
184 {
185 mt_free(mt, ws->window, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
186 }
187
188 memset(ws, 0, sizeof(*ws));
189}
190
202int welch_fft_shifted(
203 const complexf_t *x,
204 size_t n,
205 uint32_t fs,
206 welch_workspace_t *ws,
207 const float **out_freq_hz,
208 const float **out_psd_linear,
209 size_t *out_bins)
210{
211 if (!x || !ws || !out_freq_hz || !out_psd_linear || !out_bins)
212 {
213 return 0;
214 }
215
216 if (n < ws->nperseg)
217 {
218 return 0;
219 }
220
221 size_t segments = 1U + (n - ws->nperseg) / ws->step;
222 float inv_norm_fs = ws->inv_norm / ((float)fs + 1e-20f);
223 memset(ws->psd_acc, 0, ws->nperseg * sizeof(float));
224
225 for (size_t s = 0; s < segments; s++)
226 {
227 size_t start = s * ws->step;
228
229 for (size_t t = 0; t < ws->nperseg; t++)
230 {
231 size_t idx = start + t;
232 ws->fft_buf[t].re = x[idx].re * ws->window[t];
233 ws->fft_buf[t].im = x[idx].im * ws->window[t];
234 }
235
236 fft_inplace(ws->fft_buf, ws->nperseg, ws->bitrev);
237
238 for (size_t k = 0; k < ws->nperseg; k++)
239 {
240 float re = ws->fft_buf[k].re;
241 float im = ws->fft_buf[k].im;
242 float p = (re * re + im * im) * inv_norm_fs;
243 ws->psd_acc[k] += p;
244 }
245 }
246
247 for (size_t k = 0; k < ws->nperseg; k++)
248 {
249 ws->psd_acc[k] /= (float)segments;
250 }
251
252 size_t half = ws->nperseg / 2U;
253 if (!ws->freq_ready || ws->cached_fs != fs)
254 {
255 for (size_t k = 0; k < ws->nperseg; k++)
256 {
257 ws->freq_hz[k] = ((float)((int)k - (int)half) * (float)fs) / (float)ws->nperseg;
258 }
259 ws->cached_fs = fs;
260 ws->freq_ready = 1;
261 }
262
263 for (size_t k = 0; k < ws->nperseg; k++)
264 {
265 size_t src = (k + half) % ws->nperseg;
266 ws->psd_shifted[k] = ws->psd_acc[src];
267 }
268
269 *out_freq_hz = ws->freq_hz;
270 *out_psd_linear = ws->psd_shifted;
271 *out_bins = ws->nperseg;
272 return 1;
273}
274
282void compute_metrics_from_psd(
283 const float *freq_hz,
284 const float *psd_linear,
285 size_t bins,
286 iq_metrics_t *out_metrics)
287{
288 /* Metrics computed in dB domain to match Python pipeline outputs. */
289 if (!freq_hz || !psd_linear || !out_metrics || bins == 0)
290 {
291 return;
292 }
293
294 float sum_db = 0.0f;
295 size_t idx_center = bins / 2U;
296
297 for (size_t i = 0; i < bins; i++)
298 {
299 float p_db = 10.0f * log10f(psd_linear[i] + 1e-12f);
300 sum_db += p_db;
301 }
302
303 float noise_floor_dbm = sum_db / (float)bins;
304 float center_power_dbm = 10.0f * log10f(psd_linear[idx_center] + 1e-12f);
305
306 out_metrics->noise_floor_dbm = noise_floor_dbm;
307 out_metrics->center_power_dbm = center_power_dbm;
308 out_metrics->snr_center_db = center_power_dbm - noise_floor_dbm;
309}
iq_bench.h
Interfaz publica del benchmark C para procesamiento IQ Metodo 3.

Reserva memoria y actualiza contadores del tracker.

fft_inplace
static void fft_inplace(complexf_t *buf, size_t n, const size_t *bitrev)
Ejecuta FFT radix-2 in-place usando indices bit-reversal precomputados.
